name: mastquery

metadata.name: mastquery
metadata.summary: Python tools for querying the MAST Archive

documentation:
# MASTQUERY
User-friendly tools for using the MAST Mashup API (https://mast.stsci.edu/api/v0/index.html)

## Installation:

    # From PIP
    pip install mastquery
    
    # *OR* from latest version of the respository
    git clone https://github.com/gbrammer/mastquery.git
    cd mastquery
    pip install . 
    
## Demo:

See also [demo.ipynb](https://github.com/gbrammer/mastquery/blob/master/examples/demo.ipynb).

```python
>>> from mastquery import query, fetch

### Query associations
>>> tab = query.run_query(box=None, proposal_id=[11359],
                         instruments=['WFC3/IR'], 
                         filters=['G141'],
                         base_query=query.DEFAULT_QUERY)

>>> print(tab['obs_id', 'filter', 'exptime', 'proposal_id'])
  obs_id   filter  exptime  proposal_id
========   ======  =======  ===========
ib6o23010   G141    4211.7        11359

### Data products
>>> prod = query.get_products_table(tab, extensions=['RAW'])

>>> print(prod['observation_id', 'filter', 'productFilename'])
observation_id filter  productFilename  
============== ====== ==================
     ib6o23rsq   G141 ib6o23rsq_raw.fits
     ib6o23ruq   G141 ib6o23ruq_raw.fits
     ib6o23ryq   G141 ib6o23ryq_raw.fits
     ib6o23s0q   G141 ib6o23s0q_raw.fits
     
### Fetch products
>>> s3_lines = fetch.make_curl_script(prod, script_name=None, s3_sync=True)
>>> print(s3_lines[0])
aws s3 sync --request-payer requester --exclude="*.*" --include="*raw.fits" s3://stpubdata/hst/public/ib6o/ib6o23rsq/ .//
```

modules.name: setup

functions.name: mastquery.plot_utils.draw_axis_labels
documentation: Draw rounded axis labels in DMS format

functions.name: mastquery.plot_utils.insert_legacysurveys_thumbnail
documentation:
Insert thumbnail from LegacySurveys into a plot axis with ra/dec celestial
coordinates (see <https://www.legacysurvey.org/viewer/urls>)

# Parameters

ax

: Axis to hold the thumbnail

pixscale

: Pixel scale

layers

: Layer to pull. The script will cycle through `layers` until a valid
  layer is found

zorder

: zorder level on `ax` to `imshow` the thumbnail

alpha

: Transparency alpha

aspect

: Aspect ratio for `imshow`.

grayscale

: If True, plot as reverse grayscale summed from RGB thumbnail. If > 1
  then don't reverse grayscale

grayscale_params

: Scale grayscale image with
  `vmax=np.percentile(img, max_percentile)*scale_max` and
  `vmin=scale_min*vmax`

# Returns

url

: Thumbnail URL

img

: Thumbnail array

And pulls the thumbnail and inserts it into `ax`

Get recalibrated rate file if available.

functions.name: mastquery.utils.download_from_mast
documentation:
Download files from MAST API with `astroquery.mast.Observations.download_file`

# Parameters

tab

: Table from MAST API query with minimal column `dataURI`
  or `dataURL`. If a `list`, then assumes they are strings of
  `dataURL`/`dataURI` names.

path

: Output path, defaults to current working diretory

verbose

: Print status messages \[deprecated, now uses `logging`\]

overwrite

: Overwrite existing files

use_token

: Try to use a MAST token defined in a `MAST_TOKEN` environment
  variable. See <https://auth.mast.stsci.edu/info> for info about the
  token authentication.

base_url

: A base url to use when downloading. Default is the MAST Portal API

cloud_only

: See `astroquery.mast.Observations.download_file`

force_rate

: Replace 'cal' with 'rate' in filenames.

rate_ints

: Fetch `rateints` files and split into individual `rate` files

# Returns

resp

: Dict of responses from `astroquery.mast.Observations.download_file`

functions.name: mastquery.utils.old_download_from_mast
documentation:
Download files from MAST API

# Parameters

tab

: Table from MAST API query with minimal column `dataURI`
  or `dataURL`. If a `list`, then assumes they are strings of
  `dataURL`/`dataURI` names.

path

: Output path

verbose

: Print status messages \[deprecated, now uses `logging`\]

overwrite

: Overwrite existing files

min_size

: Minimum size in megabits to check if the downloaded file is rather
  an `Access Denied` file

delete_small

: Remove the file if it failed the `min_size` test

# Returns

outlist

: List of valid downloaded files

functions.name: mastquery.utils.radec_to_targname
documentation:
Turn decimal degree coordinates into a string with rounding.

Example:
    
    # Test dec: -10d10m10.10s
    >>> dec = -10. - 10./60. - 10.1/3600
    
    # Test ra: 02h02m02.20s
    >>> cosd = np.cos(dec/180*np.pi)
    >>> ra = 2*15 + 2./60*15 + 2.2/3600.*15
    
    # Round to nearest arcmin (4 seconds in RAh)
    >>> from mastquery.utils import radec_to_targname
    >>> print(radec_to_targname(ra=ra, dec=dec, round_arcsec=(4,60),
    ...                    targstr='j{rah}{ram}{ras}{sign}{ded}{dem}'))
    j020204m1010
    
    # Full precision
    >>> targstr = 'j{rah}{ram}{ras}.{rass}{sign}{ded}{dem}{des}.{dess}'
    >>> print(radec_to_targname(ra, dec,round_arcsec=(0.0001, 0.0001),
    ...                         precision=3, targstr=targstr))
    j020202.200m101010.100
    
Parameters
-----------
ra, dec : float
    Sky coordinates in decimal degrees

round_arcsec : (scalar, scalar) 
    Round the coordinates to nearest value of `round`, in arcseconds.

precision : int
    Sub-arcsecond precision, in `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord.to_string`.
    
targstr : string
    Build `targname` with this parent string.  Arguments 
    `rah, ram, ras, rass, sign, ded, dem, des, dess` are computed from the 
    (rounded) target coordinates (`ra`, `dec`) and passed to 
    `targstr.format`.

header : `~astropy.io.fits.Header`, None
    Try to get `ra`, `dec` from header keywords, first `CRVAL` and then
    `RA_TARG`, `DEC_TARG`.
    
Returns
--------
targname : str
    Target string, see the example above.

functions.name: mastquery.utils.get_mw_dust
documentation: Wrapper around functions to try to query for the MW E(B-V)

functions.name: mastquery.utils.get_irsa_dust
documentation:
Get Galactic dust reddening from NED/IRSA at a given position
http://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu/applications/DUST/docs/dustProgramInterface.html

Parameters
----------
ra, dec : float
    RA/Dec in decimal degrees.
    
type : 'SFD' or 'SandF'
    Dust model, with        
        SandF = Schlafly & Finkbeiner 2011 (ApJ 737, 103) 
          SFD = Schlegel et al. 1998 (ApJ 500, 525)

Returns
-------
ebv : float
    Color excess E(B-V), in magnitudes

modules.name: mastquery.fetch
documentation: Fetch data directly from ESA Hubble Science Archive

functions.name: mastquery.fetch.make_curl_script
documentation:
Generate a "curl" script to fetch products from the ESA HSA

Parameters
----------
table : `~astropy.table.Table`
    Table output from `~hsaquery.query` scripts.
    
level : str
    Specific data type to retrieve (e.g., 'FLT', 'RAW', 'SPT', etc.).  
    If `None`, then retrieve the following:
        'RAW'       for WFC3/IR
        'FLC'       for ACS/WFC and WFC3/UVIS
        'COM'+'C1M' for WFPC2
        
script_name : str or None
    If a string, then save the curl commands to a file.

Returns
-------
curl_list : list
    List of curl commands.

functions.name: mastquery.fetch.make_s3_command
documentation:
Generate a path to the public "STPUBDATA" S3 Hubble data mirror

# Parameters

dataset

: Dataset name, like 'IDNCM1AGQ' (or 'idncm1agq').

product

: File extension, like 'RAW' (or 'raw').

output_path

: Path where to put the file

s3_sync

: If 'cp' then run `aws s3 cp`. If anything else, run `aws s3 sync`
  excluding all files but including the desired `product`.

# Returns

cmd

: Sync/copy command for s3, e.g.,
  'aws s3 cp --request-payer requester s3://stpubdata/hst/public/idnc/idncm1agq/idncm1agq_raw.fits ./'.

:::{warning}
:::

Copying from the STPublic S3 bucket outside of AWS can incur significant
charges to an AWS account!

functions.name: mastquery.query.run_query
documentation:
Run MAST query with astroquery.mast.  

All columns listed at https://mast.stsci.edu/api/v0/_c_a_o_mfields.html 
can be used for the query.

position_box: query on s_ra / s_dec positions rather than position 
              coordinates

functions.name: mastquery.query.run_query_old
documentation:
Optional position box query:
    box = [ra, dec, radius] with ra and dec in decimal degrees and radius
    in arcminutes.

Some science observations are flagged as INTENT = Calibration, so may have
to run with extra=[] for those cases and strip out true calibs another
way.

functions.name: mastquery.jwst.query_all_jwst
documentation:
Combined query for all instruments

# Parameters

instruments

: Instruments to query (`NRC`, `NIS`, `NRS`, `MIR`)

columns

: Columns to query. If None, then get all (`'*`') columns

rd

: If a 2-tuple, then return exposures where `s_region` contains `rd`.
  If a 3-tuple, then find fields where `(targ_ra, targ_dec)` within
  `rd[2]` arcmin of `rd[0,1]`

sort

: Column for sorting the output table

fix

: Fixes to make make instrument queries more compatible with the
  high-level MAST query results (e.g., HST).

  ```pycon
  >>> mastquery.jwst.set_missing_footprints(jw)
  >>> mastquery.jwst.fix_jwst_sregions(jw)
  >>> mastquery.jwst.set_footprint_centroids(jw)
  >>> mastquery.jwst.match_dataportal_columns(jw)
  ```

kwargs

: Keyword args passed to instrument-level `mastquery.jwst.query_jwst`
  queries, e.g., `filters`, `recent_days`.

# Returns

jw

: Query result

modules.name: mastquery.overlaps
documentation: Scripts to find overlapping HST data

documentation: Save polygon summary

functions.name: mastquery.overlaps._load_poly_file
documentation: Load the saved polygon summary file

functions.name: mastquery.overlaps.find_overlaps
documentation:
Compute discrete groups from the parent table and find overlapping
datasets.

Parameters
----------
tab : `~astropy.table.Table`
    Parent table from which to compute the groups.
    
buffer_arcmin : float
    Buffer, in arcminutes, to add around the parent group polygons
    
filters : list
    List of filters to query.  If empty then return all.
    
instruments : list
    List of instruments to query.  If empty then return all.

proposid : list
    List of proposal IDs to query.  If empty then return all.

SKIP : bool
    Don't recompute if a table with the same rootname already exists.
    
extra : list
    Extra query parameters.  The available columns are described at 
    https://mast.stsci.edu/api/v0/_c_a_o_mfields.html.
    
close : bool
    If true, close the figure objects.

use_parent : bool
    Use parent table rather than performing a new query

targstr, prefix, suffix, jstr : str
    The rootname is determined from a combination of these strings, 
    with `targstr` passed to `~mastquery.utils.radec_to_targname` to 
    return a string `jname` with the final root produced with `jstr` with 
    format arguments `prefix`, `suffix` and `jname.

keep_single_name : bool
    If only a single polygon is found, try to use the RA/DEC keys of the 
    parent table metadata as the output key.
    
Returns
-------
tables : list
    
    List of grouped tables (`~astropy.table.Table`).

functions.name: mastquery.overlaps.parse_overlap_table
documentation:
Compute properties of the overlap table

Parameters
----------
tab : `~astropy.table.Table`
    Overlap table output from `find_overlaps`.
    
Returns
-------
names : list
    If `get_colnames==True`, then also return a list of the parameter
    names.

properties : list
    List of extracted properties.
